data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via learned score 0.212 (n=5.25) over codex:gpt-5.6-terra 0.000)

data.summary: Native grok WorkerAdapter shipped: auth.json-only probe (refresh_token dominates expiry), bypassPermissions dispatch with no worktree flag, parseWorkerResult reused,collectUsage deliberately absent (secret-spilling --debug-file rejected), xai tier seeds, registry append-last, and the zero-token oracle suite — full suite + tsc + coverage green, real grok neverinvoked.

data.details:
3 commit(s):
.../phases/40-native-grok-adapter/40-01-SUMMARY.md | 102 ++++++++++
 src/adapters/grok.ts                               | 125 ++++++++++++
 src/adapters/registry.ts                           |   8 +-
 src/config/config.ts                               |  10 +
 tests/adapters/grok-auth.test.ts                   | 209 +++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/adapters/grok-usage.test.ts                  |  37 ++++
 tests/adapters/real-adapters.test.ts               |   7 +-
 tests/cli/report-spend.test.ts                     |  22 +++
 tests/route/grok-routing.test.ts                   |  97 ++++++++++
 tests/route/matrix.test.ts                         |   4 +-
 10 files changed, 613 insertions(+), 8 deletions(-)

✓ src/adapters/grok.ts, registry append-last, tiers.grok seed committed; tsc clean; non-comment source free of banned tokens; real grok binary never invoked: All three artifacts in diff; npx tsc --noEmit exits 0 (verified); grok-usage.test.ts pins non-comment source free of collectUsage/--debug-file/input_tokens and passes; all grok tests mock child_process/fs — no real binary spawned.
✓ Three new zero-token oracle files committed; two pre-existing value-pins extended (never weakened); full suite + tsc + coverage floors green; real grok never invoked: grok-auth.test.ts, grok-usage.test.ts, grok-routing.test.ts are new; matrix.test.ts cheap-band pin and real-adapters.test.ts roster pin are strictly extended (grok added, nothing removed, tie-break assertion retained); npm run test:coverage exits 0 with all floors held (verified). 'Observed RED first' is a process claim the diff cannot prove, but the diff proves the substantive requirement: extension without weakening.
✓ GROK-01: grokAuthed() pure exported, auth.json ONLY, refresh_token dominates expired expires_at, fixture trap test, banner never consulted: grokAuthed(authJsonText, nowMs) is pure and exported; G1 test pins PAST expires_at + refresh_token ⇒ true (reddens on expiry-only gating); G5 asserts spawnSync never called with ['models'] during probe; probe reads ~/.grok/auth.json via readFileSync only. Tests pass (verified).
✓ GROK-02: commands pin --permission-mode bypassPermissions, shq-quoted model+prompt, never -w/--worktree, with a reddening test: Both headlessCommand and interactiveCommand contain --permission-mode bypassPermissions with shq(model)/shq(promptFile); G6 negative test asserts neither command matches /--worktree|\s-w\s/ and passes.
✓ GROK-02: parseWorkerResult recovers trailer from verbatim F-3 live capture as inline zero-token fixture; no grok-specific parser: F3_CAPTURE (verbatim 40-RESEARCH F-3, em-dash intact) parsed by parseWorkerResult in G7 with ok/summary/deviations asserted; identity pin grok.parse === parseWorkerResult proves no fork exists.
✓ GROK-03: collectUsage deliberately absent with full five-way diagnosis incl. --debug-file secret-spill (JWT + SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN) rejection; grok renders unmetered, never implicit zero: grok.ts header carries the five-way diagnosis with the --debug-file/JWT/SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN rejection verbatim; grok-usage.test.ts pins collectUsage undefined, the rationale's survival, and non-comment-source absence of any metering path; all pass.
✓ GROK-04: tiers.grok seeds grok-4.5=mid + grok-composer-2.5-fast=cheap with provenance; routing.deny removes channels; denied map pin throws RoutingError; cursor-agent:grok-4.5-xhigh and grok:grok-4.5 coexist as distinct channelKeys; vendor === 'xai' both sides: DEFAULT_CONFIG.tiers.grok seeds both models with benchmark-provenance comment; grok-routing.test.ts R1–R6 cover discovery, deny-removes-channels, RoutingError (message pins /grok:grok-4\.5/ and /routing\.deny/), channelKey coexistence, and the three-way vendor equality; all pass (verified).
✓ SC-3 falsifiable end-to-end: tickmarkr report over a token-less grok telemetry row renders `unmetered (adapter reports no usage)` and never a 0: report-spend.test.ts adds a real Journal.create + telemetry row + report() invocation asserting the unmetered line and the absence of any '0 tokens' rendering; test passes — end-to-end through the actual report command, not a claim.
✓ grok appended LAST in registry (discovery-order ties byte-identical); npm test zero-token and green; tsc clean; per-glob coverage floors hold; zero new runtime dependencies: Registry array is [claudeCode, codex, cursorAgent, opencode, pi, grok] with the order pinned in real-adapters.test.ts; the pre-existing chore→claude-code:haiku tie-break assertion in matrix.test.ts is unchanged and green; test:coverage exit 0 and tsc exit 0 verified; package.json untouched in the diff.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Minor: grok.probe() relies on probeVersion() defaulting authed:true in the success branch — it never explicitly sets authed:true when grokAuthed() passes; if probeVersion's default ever changes, grok silently reports unauthed (the G5 test would catch it, so acceptable)
- Minor: grokAuthed() treats a numeric epoch expires_at as invalid (Date.parse(String(number)) → NaN) — fine for the observed ISO-string shape (F-2), just noting the parser is format-brittle
- Minor: the worktree-ban regex /\s-w\s/ misses a trailing or leading bare -w and -w=<val>; harmless today since the commands are literals under the same test's positive pins

data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via learned score 0.212 (n=5.25) over codex:gpt-5.6-terra 0.000)

data.notes: This is a timeout on a worker making real, high-quality progress, not a stall or capability failure — the transcript shows it isolated the exact defect (grok --minimal fragments the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer across cursor-positioning escapes in the raw pty stream, so parseWorkerResult fails on the scrape while grok's session store holds the intact trailer) and was mid-way through the decisive next check: whether tickmarkr's herdr/subprocess read() returns emulated screen content (contiguous trailer) or raw pty bytes (fragmented). Retry on the same assignment with this feedback: (1) answer the read()-semantics question first by reading src/drivers/herdr.ts and subprocess.ts — if reads are screen-emulated, the default renderer likely passes and --minimal's raw-stream failure is a test-harness artifact; (2) time-box remaining probes and, above all, emit the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer — the previous attempt produced correct work but no completion marker, which is why it was flagged.

data.details:
✓ Both-mode trailer probes from a real worktree, store-bound; permission proof, compat-toggle inspection, fresh diagnosis captured; renderer feedback wired; suite green and zero-token; no credential file read: Verified independently: both session dirs exist under ~/.grok/sessions/%2Fprivate%2Ftmp%2Fgrok-live-wt-1783767686/, each summary.json info.cwd == /private/tmp/grok-live-wt-1783767686, and TICKMARKR_RESULT_p401783767686 is inside both stores (updates.jsonl + chat_history.jsonl). Permission proof (§3), compat-toggle surface (§4), and fresh diagnosis (§5) captured. Renderer feedback = new F_INT_CAPTURE fixture; no src edit needed since the default renderer passed. npm test: 797 passed / 1 skipped (the TICKMARKR_E2E gate), fake-adapter only. Evidence contains no JWT/secret content and states --debug-file was never invoked.
✓ Store-pinned live evidence committed; every machine pin cross-checks against grok's real session store; live gate green or honestly red: 40-LIVE-CHECK.md is committed at 39640f0. All four pin lines cross-checked live by the judge: both dirs exist, both cwd values match probeCwd, the nonce trailer greps inside both store dirs, and the headless session's summary.json additionally shows git_root_dir = probeCwd, the tickmarkr git remote, and head_commit 18bda32 (this run's base) — proving a genuine worktree of this repo. Verdict table marks all SCs PASS with evidence.
✓ Trailer survives BOTH dispatch modes against the REAL CLI from a REAL git worktree, real parseWorkerResult ok:true: Headless (§1) and interactive (§2) captures both carry the run nonce and parse ok:true via the real parser. The 'real git worktree' claim is machine-corroborated by the store (git_root_dir, remotes=tickmarkr repo, head_commit=base 18bda32) — not a bare /tmp dir. Interactive capture shape (spinner glyph, echoed-prompt + response occurrences, timestamp chrome) is TUI-renderer-specific, not reproducible by a -p run, and was read through tickmarkr's actual herdr recent-unwrapped path.
✓ Each capture bound to grok's OWN session store (B1): sessionId lines, dir exists, summary.json cwd == worktree realpath, trailer INSIDE store dir: All four checks pass for BOTH sessionIds, verified by the judge against the live store — dir exists, info.cwd == /private/tmp/grok-live-wt-1783767686, nonce found in chat_history.jsonl and updates.jsonl of each. A hand-typed transcript could not have planted the nonce inside grok's own store files with matching cwd/git metadata.
✓ Failure is a legal outcome (B2): honest FAIL + stop if TUI undrivable after both fallbacks: Condition never fired — the interactive half PASSED on the default renderer with no fallback needed, and the artifact explicitly records that neither --no-alt-screen nor --minimal was invoked. No synthesized capture and no human gate; the store pin substantiates the PASS.
✓ --permission-mode bypassPermissions exercised on a real tool-using task; masking config.toml state recorded verbatim with honest proof-level statement: §3 shows a real tool-forcing task (proof.txt created, exit 0, no prompt), records the verbatim `permission_mode = "always-approve"` [ui] line, and explicitly states the proof is config-masked, NOT flag-isolated, deferring isolation to an operator-run neutralized-config check — exactly the honesty the criterion demands.
✓ ~82k preamble handled by FREE half only; no --debug-file read (B3); ceiling stays in grok.ts ponytail comment; SUMMARY says not re-measured: grok.ts:42 retains the input_tokens=82874 ceiling comment (verified). §4 documents the --help/config.toml toggle inspection (none exists in 0.2.93), states --debug-file was never invoked or read, and cites only the out-of-band figure. SUMMARY states plainly it was not re-measured in-phase.
✓ GROK-03 re-confirmed against today's CLI with fresh captures: Fresh `grok update --check --json` output (updateAvailable:false, 0.2.93 stable) recorded in §5, and the zero-counter grep over the FRESH P1 session dir re-run by the judge returns 0 matches — a genuine re-probe, not copied research.
✓ npm test zero-token and green; live captures enter only as inline verbatim fixtures: Full suite: 797 passed / 1 skipped in 26s, fake-adapter only (grok binary mocked via spawnSync in the test file). F_INT_CAPTURE is an inline string constant with provenance comments; the 21 tests in grok-auth.test.ts all pass. Minor blemish: the fixture's spinner glyph is \u23af where the LIVE-CHECK shows ⠯, and one comment says '7-space margin' where the string has 5 — non-load-bearing (the trailer lines, which are the pin, match verbatim).

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- probeCwd /private/tmp/grok-live-wt-1783767686 sits under /tmp; acceptance says 'REAL git worktree (not /tmp)'. Verified it IS a genuine git worktree of this repo (git_root_dir + remotes + head_commit in grok's own summary.json), and /private/tmp is a realpath that exercises the store-keying trap, so the criterion's intent is met — but the letter is arguable and worth an orchestrator glance.
- F_INT_CAPTURE claims to be 'verbatim' but its spinner line uses \u23af (⎯) where 40-LIVE-CHECK.md shows ⠯ (braille spinner); harmless for the parser pin, inaccurate as provenance.
- F_INT_CAPTURE comment says the echoed-prompt line has a '7-space margin' but the fixture string has 5 spaces (matching the response line).
- 40-LIVE-CHECK.md §1 store pin has a garbled duplicated sentence ('NOT the burned research the burned research sessionId prefix'); cosmetic.
- 40-LIVE-CHECK.md §2 shows the parsed result without the deviations field while §1 includes it — presentation inconsistency only.
